subject: Geography
title: Megacity Pollution
description: A quick, practical guide to Megacity Pollution.

hook.text: Delhi's air in winter is sometimes more toxic than smoking 50 cigarettes a day.

buildup.text: Vehicle exhaust, factory smoke, and crop burning create a seasonal smog crisis.

discovery.text: Beijing's 'airpocalypse' in 2013 registered pollution 40 times above safe limits.

twist.text: China then invested billions in clean energy and cut Beijing's pollution by over 50% in a decade.

climax.text: Megacity pollution is solvable—but only with massive political will and investment.

punchline.text: Clean air is possible. It's just expensive.

quiz.question: How much did Beijing reduce its air pollution after investing in clean energy?
